title: Windows Internals
description: Deep Windows OS knowledge for security and RE research.

topics.title: Processes
topics.explanation:
Each process has an EPROCESS kernel structure. User-mode sees PID, handles, PEB. Tools: Process Explorer, NtQueryInformationProcess.

Games may enumerate processes for integrity checks — understand detection from a defensive research perspective.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Processes
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Processes in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Threads Deep Dive
topics.explanation: Threads share process address space but have unique stacks and TEBs. Thread Local Storage (TLS) slots store per-thread data accessed via fs:[0x58] (x64 TEB offset).
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Threads Deep Dive
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Threads Deep Dive in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Process Environment Block (PEB)
topics.description: PEB structure and module list traversal.
topics.explanation:
PEB contains:
- ImageBaseAddress
- Ldr (PEB_LDR_DATA) — loaded module linked lists
- BeingDebugged flag
- ProcessParameters

Reading PEB via GS:[0x60] (x64) reveals loaded DLLs without API calls.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Process Environment Block (PEB)

Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Thread Environment Block (TEB)
topics.explanation: TEB accessed via GS segment register (x64). Contains stack bounds, last error, TLS array, SEH chain (x86). Useful for identifying current thread context in dumps.

topics.title: System Calls
topics.explanation:
ntdll exports Nt/Zw functions that execute `syscall` instruction with service number in EAX. Direct syscalls bypass hooked ntdll (research topic for EDR analysis).

Study syscall tables for understanding API monitoring evasion *detection*.

topics.title: Handles Deep Dive
topics.explanation: Kernel object manager tracks all handles. DuplicateHandle, CloseHandle semantics. Handle grants access per ACL/security descriptor.

topics.title: Windows Drivers
topics.description: Kernel-mode driver basics for AC research.
topics.explanation:
Drivers run in ring 0 with full system access. Anti-cheat drivers monitor user-mode via callbacks (PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine, ObRegisterCallbacks).

Research focus: understand callback architecture, IOCTL communication, driver signing.

topics.title: Kernel Basics
topics.description: Ring 0/3, IRQL, and kernel debugging intro.
topics.explanation:
CPU privilege rings: ring 3 (user), ring 0 (kernel). IRQL manages interrupt priority. Kernel debugging via WinDbg + VM for driver/AC research.

Always research in isolated VMs.
